ucs.title: [LLM] LuminousMoth USB-borne propagation: signed loader + LNK pair dropped to removable media
ucs.description: Hunts LuminousMoth's signature spreading mechanism: the implant (versions tracked by Kaspersky/ESET as 'Sllauncher loader' and the Cobalt Strike stager) enumerates removable drives and writes a hidden EXE/DLL plus a weaponized LNK with a directory-icon to lure users into clicking. This USB worm-style propagation is the most distinctive LuminousMoth (Chinese-speaking, Mustang Panda-adjacent) tradecraft and is rarely produced by benign software.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_names values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created Filesystem.file_path="[D-Z]:\\*" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval has_lnk=if(match(mvjoin(file_names,"|"),"(?i)\.lnk$"),1,0), has_pe=if(match(mvjoin(file_names,"|"),"(?i)\.(exe|dll)$"),1,0), distinct_exts=mvcount(mvdedup(mvmap(file_names, lower(replace(file_names,"^.*\.",""))))) | where has_lnk=1 AND has_pe=1 AND distinct_exts>=2 | where NOT match(mvjoin(proc,"|"),"(?i)\\(explorer|setup|msiexec)\.exe$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user proc file_names file_paths count
ucs.kql:
let RemovableDrive = @"^[D-Z]:\\";
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FolderPath matches regex RemovableDrive
| extend ext = tolower(extract(@"\.([^.\\]+)$", 1, FileName))
| where ext in ("exe","dll","lnk")
| summarize Files=make_set(FileName,50), Exts=make_set(ext,10), Paths=make_set(FolderPath,20), CreatedCount=count()
    by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, bin(Timestamp,10m)
| where Exts has "lnk" and (Exts has "exe" or Exts has "dll")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("explorer.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Z]:\\"
    | extend isHidden = tobool(AdditionalFields has "\"IsHidden\":true") 
    | summarize HiddenFiles=countif(isHidden==true) by DeviceId
) on DeviceId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: LuminousMoth (G1014) is one of the very few state-aligned actors that operationalises USB worm-style propagation in 2020+ campaigns against Myanmar/Philippines/Thai government targets. The triad of (a) writes to a non-system drive letter, (b) co-creation of an LNK and a PE in the same window, (c) initiator other than explorer.exe is the precise filesystem footprint of LuminousMoth's USB component,

ucs.title: [LLM] LuminousMoth signed-binary side-loading (sllauncher.exe / winword.exe) chained to Google Drive exfiltration
ucs.description: Hunts LuminousMoth's documented stage-2 chain: a renamed/copied Microsoft-signed binary (Silverlight's sllauncher.exe, winword.exe, or WMIADAP.exe) executed from a user-writable path side-loads a malicious DLL (e.g., version.dll, wwlib.dll, msvcr100.dll), spawning a Cobalt Strike beacon that exfiltrates collected data to Google Drive (drive.google.com / googleapis.com) — a hallmark of LuminousMoth/Mustang Panda Southeast Asia operations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("sllauncher.exe","winword.exe","WMIADAP.exe") AND NOT (Processes.process_path="*\\Program Files*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\System32\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Web.url) as exfil_urls from datamodel=Web where Web.url IN ("*drive.google.com*","*docs.google.com*","*storage.googleapis.com*","*www.googleapis.com/upload*") by Web.dest Web.user _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` ] | eval suspicious_path=if(match(proc_path,"(?i)\\\\(Users|ProgramData|Public|AppData|Temp)\\\\"),1,0) | where suspicious_path=1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user process_name proc_path parent cmdline exfil_urls count
ucs.kql:
let SignedLoaders = dynamic(["sllauncher.exe","winword.exe","wmiadap.exe"]);
let SuspectPaths = @"(?i)\\(users|programdata|public|appdata|temp|perflogs)\\";
let Loaders = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ (SignedLoaders)
    | where FolderPath matches regex SuspectPaths
    | project LoaderTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessId, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let SideLoad = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SignedLoaders)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
    | where FileName in~ ("version.dll","wwlib.dll","msvcr100.dll","propsys.dll","winmm.dll","dbghelp.dll")
        or not(SHA256 in ((DeviceImageLoadEvents | where FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\" | distinct SHA256)))
    | project SideloadTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DllName=FileName, DllPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessId;
let GDriveExfil = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("drive.google.com","docs.google.com","storage.googleapis.com","www.googleapis.com")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe")
    | project ExfilTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId;
Loaders
| join kind=inner (SideLoad) on DeviceId
| where ProcessId == InitiatingProcessId and SideloadTime between (LoaderTime .. (LoaderTime + 5m))
| join kind=inner (GDriveExfil) on DeviceId
| where ExfilTime between (LoaderTime .. (LoaderTime + 1h))
| project LoaderTime, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, DllName, DllPath, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.rationale: Public reporting (Kaspersky GReAT 2021, ESET, ThreatBook) shows LuminousMoth specifically abuses sllauncher.exe (Microsoft Silverlight) and winword.exe staged in user-writable paths to side-load malicious DLLs, and exfiltrates browser cookies and documents directly to Google Drive — not via the user's browser process. Correlating signed-loader-from-user-path → non-system DLL load → non-browser pro
